title: School Hymn (Lord of our life)
history: The traditional foundation hymn sung by all Rathkeale students during formal assemblies and chapel services.

title: Jerusalem
lyrics:
And did those feet in ancient time
Walk upon England's mountains green?
And was the holy Lamb of God
On England's pleasant pastures seen?
And did the countenance divine
Shine forth upon our clouded hills?
And was Jerusalem builded here
Among these dark satanic mills?

Bring me my bow of burning gold!
Bring me my arrows of desire!
Bring me my spear! O clouds unfold!
Bring me my chariot of fire!
I will not cease from mental fight,
Nor shall my sword sleep in my hand,
Till we have built Jerusalem
In England's green and pleasant land.
history: A powerful anthem traditionally sung with immense pride and energy during Friday house singing sessions.

title: Rathkeale School Song
lyrics:
To the hills we lift our eyes,
Where the golden sunbeams rise.
Standing proud and standing true,
We pledge our hearts, our strength to you.
Through the years our voices ring,
Of loyalty and truth we sing.

In the classroom, on the field,
To our challenges we never yield.
Hearts together, side by side,
Walking with Rathkeale pride.
With our green and with our red,
By our ancient values led.
history: Composed to celebrate the unique environmental surroundings, values, and strong community spirit of the college.

title: Abide With Me
history: A deeply respected hymn utilized during traditional evening chapel services and solemn school events.

title: Guide Me, O Thou Great Redeemer
history: A classic, soaring hymn often selected for major school calendar operations and public community services.

title: Amazing Grace
lyrics:
Amazing grace! How sweet the sound
That saved a wretch like me!
I once was lost, but now am found;
Was blind, but now I see.

'Twas grace that taught my heart to fear,
And grace my fears relieved;
How precious did that grace appear
The hour I first believed.

Through many dangers, toils and snares,
I have already come;
'Tis grace hath brought me safe thus far,
And grace will lead me home.

When we've been there ten thousand years,
Bright shining as the sun,
We've no less days to sing God's praise
Than when we've first begun.
history: A universally recognized hymn included for reflection and special combined college services.

title: How Great Thou Art
lyrics:
O Lord, my God, when I in awesome wonder
Consider all the worlds Thy Hands have made;
I see the stars, I hear the rolling thunder,
Thy power throughout the universe displayed.

Then sings my soul, my Saviour God, to Thee,
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!
Then sings my soul, my Saviour God, to Thee,
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!

When Christ shall come with shout of acclamation
And take me home, what joy shall fill my heart!
Then I shall bow in humble adoration,
And there proclaim: 'My God, how great Thou art!'
history: A powerful piece with deep structural dynamics frequently used to open formal prize-giving events.

title: Be Thou My Vision
history: An ancient traditional melody used during quiet Sunday evening chapel services for boarders.

title: God Defend New Zealand
lyrics:
God of Nations at Thy feet,
In the bonds of love we meet,
Hear our voices, we entreat,
God defend our free land.
Guard Pacific's triple star,
From the shafts of strife and war,
Make her praises heard afar,
God defend New Zealand.

E Ihowā Atua,
O ngā iwi mātou rā
Āta whakarangona;
Me aroha noa
Kia hua ko te pai;
Kia tau tō atawhai;
Manaakitia mai
Aotearoa.
history: The national anthem, practiced diligently to ensure accurate bilingual pronunciation during inter-school fixtures.

title: Swing Low, Sweet Chariot
lyrics:
Swing low, sweet chariot,
Coming for to carry me home,
Swing low, sweet chariot,
Coming for to carry me home.

I looked over Jordan, and what did I see
Coming for to carry me home?
A band of angels coming after me,
Coming for to carry me home.

If you get there before I do,
Coming for to carry me home,
Tell all my friends I'm coming too,
Coming for to carry me home.
history: A traditional anthem often utilized to build camaraderie and unified school pride at major sporting exchanges.

title: Lord of the Dance
lyrics:
I danced in the morning when the world was begun,
And I danced in the moon and the stars and the sun,
And I came down from heaven and I danced on the earth,
At Bethlehem I had my birth.

Dance, then, wherever you may be,
I am the Lord of the Dance, said he,
And I'll lead you all, wherever you may be,
And I'll lead you all in the Dance, said he.
history: A popular 1963 English hymn written by Sydney Carter, adapting the melody of the Shaker song 'Simple Gifts'.

title: Take Me Home, Country Roads
lyrics:
Almost heaven, West Virginia,
Blue Ridge Mountains, Shenandoah River.
Life is old there, older than the trees,
Younger than the mountains, blowing like a breeze.

Country roads, take me home,
To the place I belong,
West Virginia, mountain mama,
Take me home, country roads.
history: The legendary 1971 country-folk anthem written by John Denver, Bill Danoff, and Taffy Nivert.

title: Where the Road Runs Out (And the Signposts End)
lyrics:
Where the road runs out and the signposts end,
We will find our way with a trusted friend.
Through the mist and rain, under stormy skies,
We will watch the dawn and the sunbeams rise.
history: A classic, resonant piece depicting journeying, exploration, and discovery at the margins of map borders.

title: Viva La Vida
lyrics:
I used to rule the world,
Seas would rise when I gave the word.
Now in the morning I sleep alone,
Sweep the streets I used to own.

I hear Jerusalem bells a-ringing,
Roman Cavalry choirs are singing.
Be my mirror, my sword and shield,
My missionaries in a foreign field.
history: Released by Coldplay in 2008, featuring historical baroque themes and looping orchestral string textures.

title: Let It Be
lyrics:
When I find myself in times of trouble, Mother Mary comes to me,
Speaking words of wisdom, let it be.
And in my hour of darkness she is standing right in front of me,
Speaking words of wisdom, let it be.

Let it be, let it be, let it be, let it be,
Whisper words of wisdom, let it be.
history: The iconic 1970 title track gospel-rock ballad recorded by The Beatles, written by Paul McCartney.

title: All Things Bright and Beautiful
lyrics:
All things bright and beautiful,
All creatures great and small,
All things wise and wonderful,
The Lord God made them all.

Each little flower that opens,
Each little bird that sings,
He made their glowing colours,
He made their tiny wings.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. A well-known children's hymn written by Cecil Frances Alexander in 1848, often used in junior assemblies.

title: All Creatures of Our God and King
lyrics:
All creatures of our God and King,
Lift up your voice and with us sing,
Alleluia! Alleluia!
Thou burning sun with golden beam,
Thou silver moon with softer gleam,
O praise Him! O praise Him!
Alleluia! Alleluia! Alleluia!
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. A traditional hymn adapted from the writings of St. Francis of Assisi, sung at major college thanksgiving services.

title: Onward, Christian Soldiers
lyrics:
Onward, Christian soldiers, marching as to war,
With the cross of Jesus going on before.
Christ, the royal Master, leads against the foe;
Forward into battle, see His banners go!
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. A rousing Victorian-era hymn frequently chosen for processional entrances at formal college ceremonies.

title: House Spirit Anthem
lyrics:
Stand together, shoulder to shoulder,
Colours flying high and bold.
Every voice a little louder,
Every story to be told.

This is our house, this is our pride,
We rise as one, side by side.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. An original composition created for inter-house competition days to rally student spirit.

title: Old Boys' Reunion Song
lyrics:
Back to the halls where we first began,
Back to the friendships, woman and man.
Years may pass but the bond remains,
Echoes of laughter, echoes of names.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. A nostalgic piece performed each year at the alumni reunion dinner to honour past students.

title: Morning Has Broken
lyrics:
Morning has broken, like the first morning,
Blackbird has spoken, like the first bird.
Praise for the singing, praise for the morning,
Praise for the springing fresh from the world.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. A gentle hymn, popularised by Cat Stevens, often sung at sunrise chapel services.

title: First XV Anthem
lyrics:
Forward together, the scrum holds firm,
Every tackle, every turn.
Green and red against the sky,
We fight as one, we will not lie down.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. A high-energy chant composed for the First XV rugby team, sung from the sidelines at home matches.

title: The Lord's My Shepherd
lyrics:
The Lord's my Shepherd, I'll not want;
He makes me down to lie
In pastures green; He leadeth me
The quiet waters by.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. A Scottish metrical psalm setting, traditionally included in remembrance and memorial services.

title: Founder's Day Hymn
lyrics:
We gather here in gratitude,
For those who built this place with care.
Their vision lives within these walls,
Their spirit moves within the air.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. Composed specifically for the annual Founder's Day service, honouring the school's establishment.

title: Ode to Joy
lyrics:
Joyful, joyful, we adore Thee,
God of glory, Lord of love;
Hearts unfold like flowers before Thee,
Opening to the sun above.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. Adapted from Beethoven's Ninth Symphony, used as a celebratory hymn at end-of-year prize giving.

title: Graduation Farewell Song
lyrics:
As we step beyond these gates today,
We carry all you gave us on our way.
Thank you for the lessons and the light,
We leave as one, we leave tonight.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. Sung each year by graduating seniors at the leaving assembly, marking the end of their school journey.

title: Deck the Halls
lyrics:
Deck the halls with boughs of holly,
Fa la la la la, la la la la.
'Tis the season to be jolly,
Fa la la la la, la la la la.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. A traditional Welsh carol performed each year at the college's end-of-term Christmas service.

title: First Eight Rowing Anthem
lyrics:
Pull together, blade through water,
Every stroke a step closer to home.
Eight as one upon the river,
We row on, we row alone no longer.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. Composed for the rowing squad, traditionally sung at the regatta send-off before major races.

title: Now Thank We All Our God
lyrics:
Now thank we all our God,
With hearts and hands and voices,
Who wondrous things hath done,
In whom His world rejoices.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. A German Reformation-era hymn traditionally sung at the college's harvest and thanksgiving services.

title: House Cup Victory Song
lyrics:
Raise the cup up to the sky,
We worked together, you and I.
Every point and every try,
Now we sing it, loud and high.
history: PLACEHOLDER ENTRY — replace audioUrl with a real hosted file. Performed at the end-of-year House Cup ceremony to celebrate the winning house.
